title: Govt got 'Satluj' pulled off OTT on security grounds; 'encounter' victims kin oppose film ban
content: The government asked ZEE5 to remove the film 'Satluj' citing security concerns.  This action followed the movie's release on the OTT platform after a long delay.  The film depicts the story of activist Jaswant Singh Khalra and his disappearance.  Its makers released the movie on ZEE5 with a new name after censor objections.  Families of victims oppose the ban, believing the film acknowledges a painful history.

title: 'Bibi made rosy predictions': US miffed at Netanyahu's promises about Iran war
content: A senior Trump administration official said Netanyahu had repeatedly made optimistic predictions about the course of the war that ultimately failed to materialise. The official's comments reflected growing scepticism within the administration over Netanyahu's assessments of the conflict and signalled that Washington intends to independently verify future claims before acting on them.

title: Kerala man held under UAPA for posting comment supporting Pahalgam attack
content: Authorities have detained Mohammed Sanoop under the stringent provisions of UAPA for allegedly posting an anti-national comment on social media while abroad. His arrest occurred as he returned from Saudi Arabia, prompted by a serious investigation into the matter. Following the proceedings, the court has placed him in judicial custody as the case unfolds.

title: After all the drama... Balogun's controversial FIFA reprieve ends with little impact
content: Belgium decisively defeated the United States 4-1 in the World Cup Round of 16. Folarin Balogun, whose suspension was controversially overturned, struggled to impact the match. He registered few touches and failed to score during the game. The United States faced elimination in the Round of 16 once again. This defeat concluded their home World Cup aspirations after much scrutiny.

title: 'History points to downside': Michael Burry warns on Micron after Trump-linked donations
content: Renowned investor Michael Burry has initiated a short position against Micron Technology, citing 'historically extreme' stock valuations driven by AI hype and investor FOMO, not fundamentals.  Despite a recent $250 million donation to a children's savings scheme, Burry warns of significant downside due to the memory chipmaker's cyclical nature and poor profitability metrics.  This move aligns with his broader bearish outlook on AI semiconductor stocks.

title: Wife, lover held for killing Gulf returnee, death staged as accidental fall
content: Police uncovered an alleged murder plot by a wife and her associates. The victim was initially reported to have fallen from a cot. Sedatives and cleaning liquid were allegedly administered through a cannula. The accused attempted to pass the death off as an accident. Three individuals, including the wife, were arrested for the crime.

title: Tears and applause: Cristiano Ronaldo bows out of his final World Cup with a heartbreak
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's illustrious World Cup journey came to an emotional end as Portugal faced defeat against Spain. While sadness enveloped him, he remained proud of his legacy, being the sole player to score in six different World Cups. Having secured three titles with Portugal, including the 2016 Euros, he announced this bittersweet event as his last World Cup appearance, marking the conclusion of an era.

title: Jamie Dimon gives a 'reality check' to Canada's PM Mark Carney on his unity call to Europe
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has dismissed Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's call for middle powers to unite, calling it a "fantasy." Dimon pointed to Europe's economic struggles as an example of cooperation's limitations, citing high taxes and regulation. He emphasized that attractive business environments are crucial for growth, contrasting Europe's declining competitiveness with the U.S. market. Dimon also reassured investors about America's investment appeal, noting China's evolving global approach.

title: Bengaluru couple kill 11-month-old daughter, pass off death as cot fall
content: Avalahalli police arrested Shekhappa for his infant daughter's murder.  The child's death was not accidental, as initial reports suggested.  Investigations revealed injuries inconsistent with a fall from a cot.  The post-mortem indicated extensive internal injuries and respiratory failure.  Shekhappa's wife, Vijayalakshmi, is currently absconding from authorities.

title: Luxury brand 'creates' foldable iPhone Ultra in 24K gold, some silver, and a lot more
content: Caviar has unveiled luxury versions of Apple's rumored foldable iPhone Ultra.  These custom devices feature 24K gold and genuine crocodile leather.  The "Flagship" collection offers four distinct design solutions for the upcoming phone.  Technical specifications are based on rumors, including a titanium body and advanced cameras.  Pre-orders for these exclusive reimagined iPhones start at $13,840.

title: FIFA World Cup: Belgium march into quarter-finals after ruthless 4-1 demolition of USA
content: Belgium ended the United States’ World Cup 2026 dream with a ruthless 4-1 victory in Seattle, reaching the quarter-finals after punishing costly defensive errors. Charles De Ketelaere scored twice, while Hans Vanaken and Romelu Lukaku completed the rout. Despite dominating possession, Mauricio Pochettino’s hosts fell short of a first World Cup quarter-final since 2002.

title: New Army chief’s 'VIJAY' mantra: How India’s land force is preparing for future wars
content: Army Chief General Dhiraj Seth has unveiled his "VIJAY" mantra, emphasizing Vigilance, Innovation, Jointness, Atmanirbharta, and Yodha First. This strategic direction aligns with the army's Decade of Transformation and Prime Minister Modi's "JAI" directive for integrated defence forces. The initiative aims to bolster capabilities through modernization and doctrinal shifts, preparing the army for future challenges in a rapidly evolving global security landscape.

title: Luka Doncic leaves LeBron James behind, makes surprising Nikola Jokić pick in rapid-fire
content: Luka Dončić turned heads by repeatedly choosing Nikola Jokić over LeBron James and other NBA legends in a rapid-fire "ultimate teammate" challenge. The viral clip surfaced shortly after James' Lakers exit, fueling fan speculation. While reports indicate no tension between the former teammates, many viewed Dončić's choice as a reflection of ideal 2-on-2 chemistry rather than a slight against James.

title: Blake Lively, Ryan Reynolds reportedly devastated over Swift-Kelce wedding snub
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce just had the wedding of the year as billions across the world cheered them on throughout the world across countries. While the wedding pictures have been kept strictly private, Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds remained missing from the party. At a time when Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce publicly distanced themselves from the Hollywood power couple, it seems both Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds are furious at this.

title: Who is Erling Haaland's sister? Gabrielle goes viral at FIFA World Cup 2026
content: Erling Haaland has become one of the biggest stories of the FIFA World Cup 2026. While the Norway striker has impressed on the pitch, one of his family members has unexpectedly grabbed attention off it. His older sister, Gabrielle Braut Haaland, has gone viral after cameras spotted her cheering from the stands.

title: What sparked Kylian Mbappe-Celeste Amarilla World Cup controversy?
content: France’s World Cup win over Paraguay was overshadowed by a racism controversy involving Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla. After a tense post-match incident between Kylian Mbappe and Orlando Gill, Amarilla posted offensive remarks about the France star on X. Mbappe responded publicly, condemning the comments and defending Paraguay’s players while calling out racism and hatred.

title: Amitabh Bachchan called at 3 am to buy Ayodhya land, transferred Rs 15 crore next day
content: Amitabh Bachchan personally called The House of Abhinandan Lodha founder and chairman Abhinandan Lodha at around 3 am to enquire about buying land in Ayodhya and transferred Rs 15 crore the very next day after learning the price, the real estate entrepreneur has revealed. Bachchan has steadily expanded his real estate portfolio in Ayodhya over the past few years.

title: 'Despicable woman': Kylian Mbappé explodes at Paraguay senator over racist attack
content: French captain Kylian Mbappé condemned racist social media posts by a Paraguayan senator.  The senator's remarks targeted Mbappé's background and appearance after a World Cup match.  Paraguay's government and the French Football Federation also denounced the senator's unacceptable comments.  French officials, including President Macron, voiced strong support for Mbappé and condemned racism.  This controversy follows similar remarks made by a former Paraguayan goalkeeper before the game.

title: 'Euro is just as significant as World Cup': Ronaldo sparks debate after Portugal exit
content: Cristiano Ronaldo stated Portugal's Euro 2016 win holds immense personal value. He expressed disappointment after Portugal's World Cup exit, his final campaign. Ronaldo affirmed he gave his best and has no regrets about his efforts. He won three titles for Portugal, ending their trophy drought. The Euro 2016 victory remains a defining achievement in his career.

title: 'He will kill me': Delhi newlywed told mother days before fatal fall; husband held
content: A newlywed woman died after allegedly falling from a building in Delhi. Her family accused her husband and relatives of dowry harassment and abuse. The woman had reportedly told her mother about physical beatings and fears for her life. Police have arrested her husband and registered a dowry death case. Investigations are ongoing into the circumstances surrounding her death.

title: UP man kills girlfriend for refusing to show WhatsApp chats
content: A man allegedly murdered and dismembered his girlfriend after suspecting her of phone infidelity. Police arrested the 42-year-old boyfriend, Hemal Khakharia, following a shootout and encounter. The victim's body parts were found stuffed in black plastic bags along a roadside. Khakharia confessed to the crime after being apprehended with the remaining body parts. The victim, a married woman, had met Khakharia through social media two months prior.

title: Ram Temple ex-trustee Mishra failed to secure donations: SIT
content: A preliminary SIT report details alleged embezzlement of Ram temple donations. Senior Trust functionary Anil Mishra's oversight failure enabled suspects to pilfer funds. Nearly seventy instances of alleged pilferage were captured on CCTV footage. Subhash Srivastava, the counting room in charge, also faces serious lapses. The report names six suspects involved in theft and misappropriation offenses.

title: Oil prices inch higher as focus shifts to supply, demand and Hormuz shipments
content: Oil prices saw a slight increase Tuesday after a tanker was struck near the Strait of Hormuz.  This incident raised security concerns on a vital global oil shipping route.  However, market gains were limited as traders focused on rising crude supplies.  The United Arab Emirates and OPEC+ have agreed to increase their oil production levels.  Saudi Arabia also lowered its August crude selling price for Asian markets.

title: Ronaldo breaks down in tears as Portugal World Cup dream ends - Watch
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's FIFA World Cup journey concluded with Portugal's Round of 16 elimination.  He expressed sadness and confirmed this was his final tournament appearance.  Ronaldo stated he gave his best and leaves with a clear conscience.  Portugal coach Roberto Martinez praised his influence and legacy in football.  Ronaldo's career includes major international titles and scoring records.

title: Pune-Mumbai Expressway ‘connecting link’ goes back to being ‘missing link’ for 18 hours after landslide
content: A major landslide on the Pune-Mumbai Missing Link caused an eighteen-hour traffic shutdown. Nearly one hundred tonnes of mud and rock fell onto the road near tunnel two. The incident occurred after rainfall three times the average in twenty-four hours. Officials are consulting IIT-Bombay for a technical assessment of the route. Public safety remains the highest priority for the MSRDC.

title: Ancient bees built nests inside the tooth sockets of fossilised animal jaws
content: Remarkable fossil evidence reveals ancient bees, 20,000 years old, nested within empty tooth sockets of fossilized animal jaws in a Hispaniola cave.  Researchers discovered these unique nests, classified as a new trace fossil, indicating solitary bees ingeniously repurposed bone cavities for their eggs. This unexpected behavior highlights the adaptability of bees and suggests similar discoveries may await in existing fossil collections.

title: E20 fuel may damage rubber parts in E10 cars, said unpublished report
content: An ARAI report noted rubber part deterioration in E10 vehicles using E20 fuel. Metallic components showed no adverse impact, and emissions remained within legislative limits. One BS-VI turbocharged engine experienced issues after durability testing, unlike a BS-IV engine. Two-wheeler engine durability tests showed acceptable performance with E20 fuel. Fuel consumption increased by two to six percent for vehicles using E20.

title: NFL trade news: Cowboys-Chiefs $6 million safety deal finally revealed
content: The Dallas Cowboys nearly completed a draft-day trade with the Kansas City Chiefs to move up for safety Caleb Downs, head coach Brian Schottenheimer revealed. The Chiefs instead traded to No. 6 for Mansoor Delane, forcing Dallas to adjust and trade from No. 12 to No. 11, where they still selected Downs. Schottenheimer said Downs topped the Cowboys' draft board, while the rookie is already being tipped as a breakout player entering training camp.

title: 'Alpha' hits Rs 63.64 crore worldwide on first Monday; eyes Rs 100 crore mark
content: Alpha continues its steady box office run after a strong opening weekend. The film collected an estimated Rs 3.85 crore net on its fourth day. Its total India gross collection reached an estimated Rs 45.34 crore. Overseas earnings pushed the worldwide gross to an estimated Rs 63.64 crore. The movie is now on its way to the Rs 100 crore milestone.

title: 'Integral part of India': Diplomat protests wrong depiction of J&K at Dhaka seminar
content: India lodged a protest at a Dhaka seminar after a map showed Kashmir as part of Pakistan.  An Indian diplomat immediately raised objections during the presentation by a former ambassador.  The diplomat stated that Jammu and Kashmir is an integral part of India.  The former ambassador claimed the map was for representational purposes only.  The seminar was organized by the Bangladesh Institute of International and Strategic Studies.

title: Non-stop rain cuts off Mumbai as rail, highway traffic hit; schools, colleges shut today
content: Mumbai experienced severe disruptions due to heavy rains and strong winds for the third consecutive day. Torrential showers also affected other regions, with Palghar district being the worst hit. Schools and colleges were declared closed in Mumbai, Thane, Palghar, and Raigad due to the ongoing weather warnings. Transport services faced significant disruptions, including train cancellations and highway closures due to landslides and waterlogging.

title: Taj Mahal originally temple? HC seeks response of Centre, ASI on inspection plea
content: The Allahabad High Court has directed the central government and ASI to file responses. This follows a petition challenging an Agra court's refusal to allow a Taj Mahal survey. Petitioners claim the monument is an ancient Hindu temple called Tejo Mahalaya. They seek permission to perform prayers and conduct a survey. The court is asked to set aside lower court orders and decide the application.

title: 'Resigned judge' can't be impeached, say constitutional experts
content: Parliament will table an inquiry report on former judge Yashwant Varma's removal.  Justice Varma resigned in April after facing allegations of unaccounted money.  Constitutional experts state impeachment proceedings are now pointless after his resignation.  They emphasize a judge ceases to hold office upon resignation, not requiring acceptance.  The report's tabling is seen as a political formality with no effective purpose.

title: Saudi Arabia effects deepest crude price cut in 26 years
content: Saudi Arabia significantly reduced crude oil prices, reflecting intense competition among producers. Major Persian Gulf nations are increasing their oil output, restoring pre-war export levels. This move coincides with OPEC+ allies agreeing to further boost production targets for August. Lower oil prices and increased supply will benefit Indian refiners and reduce government subsidy burdens. Reduced energy costs for Indian companies will help control inflation and support consumer spending.

title: Viral post: Same medicines cost Rs 2,500 in India vs Rs 42,000 in US
content: An Indian senior citizen's account of navigating the US healthcare system has gone viral after an X user Adhir Sinha shared it on the social media platform. The post claimed that medicines costing around Rs 2,500 in India ended up costing the equivalent of Rs 42,000 in the US, sparking a debate about healthcare costs and everyday conveniences in India and the United States.

title: 1847 discovery: Chlorine handwashing cut childbirth deaths 90%, doctors ignored it
content: In a radical move, Ignaz Semmelweis mandated handwashing in hospitals, drastically reducing maternal mortality rates. Despite evidence supporting his approach, he faced fierce opposition from fellow medical professionals, as he couldn't provide a scientific rationale for his findings. Much later, the establishment of germ theory validated his revolutionary work, leading to fundamental changes in medical practices that ultimately saved innumerable lives.

title: NIA charges LeT chief Hafiz Saeed with plotting Pahalgam terror strike
content: The National Investigation Agency has charged Hafiz Saeed for the Pahalgam attack.  This attack tragically killed twenty-five tourists and one local resident.  Evidence includes digital trails and on-ground examination by the agency.  Saeed, LeT chief, faces charges under new Indian penal codes.  He will be tried in absentia for waging war against India.

title: Drunken brawl leads to chopping down of two fully grown trees in Hyderabad
content: The trees in question — a 40-45 years old peepal,  and a 20-25 years old jamun — were in the middle of 40-feet road in RTC Colony, Chintalkunta in the Malkajgiri Municipal Corporation’s purview

title: About 100 people from Karnataka in attendance at Ayatollah Khamenei’s funeral in Tehran
content: Many attendees, including students and professionals already based in Iran, traveled on a chartered flight from Bengaluru, drawn by a deep personal connection to Khamenei, who had previously visited their village.

title: POCSO pendency and torturous process further victimise children subjected to sexual abuse
content: A staggering 18,733 POCSO cases are pending in various courts across the 38 districts in Tamil Nadu. The situation is exacerbated by patchy support services for children who opt to go through the procedural justice system

title: Chief Minister Joseph Vijay writes to Prime Minister Narendra Modi opposing proposed amendment to Food Security Act
content: In letter to PM, he urges the Centre to retain the existing entitlement of 35 kg of foodgrains per month for every household covered under the Antyodaya Anna Yojana, irrespective of family size

title: Chief Minister Vijay visits desalination plants and drinks water
content: An official release said that Mr. Vijay directed officials to ensure proper maintenance of the Nemmeli desalination plants so that drinking water is supplied to the public without interruption

title: Three held for Baruipur rape and murder; govt. will ensure capital punishment is awarded, says CM
content: There may be a communal angle to the lynching of a man in the unrest after the incident, Suvendu Adhikari says; police form a SIT, register four cases

title: WhatsApp gets more time to reply to username notice, assures no India rollout till talks end
content: Meta-owned WhatsApp has been granted a three-day extension to respond to a government notice regarding its controversial username feature, which allows users to communicate without sharing phone numbers.

title: Form used in rehearsal for second phase of Census has an ‘open column’ to record caste
content: Final methodology to enumerate caste as part of Census 2027 will be prepared based on the feedback received during the test exercise; the open column methodology had resulted in the 2011 caste survey returning over 46 lakh different ‘caste names’

title: Controversial book: police search Samagra Shiksha office in Jammu
content: Action part of an ongoing investigation to identify people responsible for the book Great Personalities and Legends of J&K, which has profiled separatist leaders such as Maqbool Bhat, Masarat Alam, Shabir Shah and Syed Ali Geelani

title: Odisha SIR: Malkangiri records 27,653 deletions, highest in the State
content: Ganjam, which sends the highest number of migrant labourers to different parts of country and outside, has the highest deletion of 2,07,626 voters followed by Cuttack district with 1,55,164.

title: Amit Shah lays foundation stone for statue of Syama Prasad Mookerjee in Kolkata
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Home Minister Amit Shah lauded the contribution of the Jana Sangh founder in nation building on his 125th birth anniversary

title: Like Jammu, lotus will bloom in Kashmir too: BJP chief Nitin Nabin
content: Nabin’s visit to Jammu and Kashmir comes on the 125th birth anniversary of Jana Sangh founder Syama Prasad Mookerjee

title: Seafarers to undergo mandatory yoga-based wellness training
content: Govt. requires all seafarers holding a valid Indian Continuous Discharge Certificate (CDC) to complete the course within one year, and mandates that all candidates at DGS-approved Maritime Training Institutes (MTIs) finish the modules

title: Madhya Pradesh govt. reconstitutes Waqf Board under new law, inclusion of Hindu members draws criticism
content: Congress MLA Arif Masood terms the move ‘completely wrong’ as the matter related to various provisions of the new Act is pending before the Supreme Court

title: Won’t allow govt control of Ayodhya Ram temple at any cost, says VHP
content: But it has no objection to appointing a professional Chief Executive Officer, provided the person is not a serving bureaucrat and is accountable to Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust

title: Modi begins three-nation trip, starts with Indonesia to boost strategic ties
content: Prime Minister set to hold discussions with Indonesian President Subianto to add ‘momentum to this partnership across diverse sectors’; he will travel to Australia and New Zealand next as part of efforts to promote a free and open Indo-Pacific

title: Bikers perform wheelies in front of ambulance, endanger patient’s life in Bengaluru
content: According to witnesses, the riders were not wearing helmets nor were number plates visible in the two-wheelers

title: Residents flag dangerous road conditions across East Bengaluru’s IT corridor
content: These roads serve major residential areas, IT corridor, schools and hospitals but are filled with potholes and pose a risk to motorists

title: Safe Footpath Campaign runs into conflict with street vendors in Bengaluru
content: Hundreds of street vendors have been evicted and the wares they were hawking seized, as part of the drive in the past one week.

title: MBA felicitates Justice V. Mohana, first woman from Tamil Nadu to be elevated directly to Supreme Court
content: She also inaugurates an endowment lecture series and hails the contributions of M.K. Nambyar, V.P. Raman, R. Krishnamoorthy and M. Subramaniam to the march of law

title: SIT probe reveals police conspiracy in Sai Krishna custodial death case
content: According to the investigating officers, the accused allegedly planned a conspiracy and attempted to create a story that the deceased had escaped from police custody and travelled to Goa, where his mobile phone signals were traced

title: Cosmoserve’s active debris removal tech to take flight with Skyroot’s Vikram
content: First demonstration of soft robotic capture in orbit using the technology that went from concept to flight-ready hardware stage in four months is to be undertaken

title: Biggest Hamas Decision in 20 Years Leaves One Critical Question Unanswered
content: For almost 20 years Hamas has combined armed resistance with day-to-day administration in the Gaza Strip. It has operated ministries, schools, municipal services and health institutions while simultaneously maintaining an armed organisation.

title: Delhi Doctor Dies By Suicide At Hospital After 'Family Opposed Marriage'
content: Doctor on night duty found dead at Delhi hospital; suicide note reveals emotional distress over marriage.

title: After US-Iran Deal, Pakistan Turns Peacemaker For Fractured Libya: Report
content: A summary of a proposed "Libya Reunification Plan" showed it would set out a 36-month transitional power-sharing arrangement under a body called the Government of National Consensus and Presidential Council.

title: Weather Today Live: Mumbai Rain Fury Continues For 3rd Day, Emergency Teams Deployed Across City
content: Mumbai, Delhi-NCR Monsoon Alert Live Updates: Special teams have been stationed at various vulnerable locations to provide immediate responses to emergencies, including tree collapses, short circuits, and partial or complete cave-ins of dilapidated buildings.

title: Pink Autos Steer Change In Chennai, While Offering Safer Rides To Passengers
content: Launched amid growing concerns over crimes against women, the Pink Auto initiative seeks to improve women's safety while simultaneously transforming the lives of women through financial independence.

title: Oil Tanker Hit By Unknown Missile In Strait Of Hormuz: UK Maritime Agency
content: An "unknown projectile" struck and caused a fire on an oil tanker off the coast of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, British maritime security agency UKMTO said.

title: US Concerned By China Nuclear Buildup After Missile Launch
content: The US said Monday that China's "rapid and opaque" nuclear weapons buildup "is of great concern" after Beijing test-fired of a missile from a submarine into the Pacific Ocean.

title: Ukraine War Resolution "Closer" After Talks With Putin, Zelensky: Trump
content: US President Donald Trump said on Monday that a resolution to the more than four-year-old war in Ukraine is "getting closer than people realize" and that he will talk about Ukraine during talks in Turkey this week at a NATO summit.

title: US Court Rejects Elon Musk Bid To Set Aside Twitter Fraud Verdict
content: Investors accused Musk of falsely suggesting that Twitter was overrun by fake and spam accounts, known as bots, to force the company to renegotiate his offer or let him back out. Th

title: Island-Wide Blackout Hits Cuba As Its Fuel Reserve Dwindles
content: Cuba produces only 40% of the fuel it needs, while the 730,000 barrels of oil delivered by a Russian tanker in late March ran out by the end of April.

title: Trump's Fireworks Made Washington World's Most Polluted City
content: July 4 fell during a record-breaking heat wave that upended the parades, block parties and barbecues that traditionally mark the day the United States celebrates its break from Britain.

title: Trump's "Finish The Job" Warning To Iran Amid Peace Talks
content: Donald Trump on Monday said the United States would either reach a deal with Iran or "finish the job", while asserting that Washington has the capability to cripple Tehran's critical infrastructure if it chooses to do so.

title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 3,535, Over 16,700 Injured
content: The death toll from Venezuela's twin earthquakes has risen to 3,535, with more than 16,700 injured in the disaster, according to updated official figures released on Monday.

title: Prince Harry Arrives In UK Amid Security Spat
content: Prince Harry arrived in Britain on Monday for a five-day visit expected to go ahead mostly without his wife and children after they were reportedly refused police protection.

title: Xi Jinping To Visit US In September, Says Trump
content: The President said the White House has struggled for decades to accommodate large state events, noting that the Executive Mansion currently lacks a dedicated ballroom.

title: Mourners Throng Funeral Procession In Tehran For Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
content: Khamenei's flag-draped coffin, and those of his family members who were killed Feb. 28 in an airstrike at the start of the war launched by Israel and the United States, sat aboard a truck decorated to resemble the ornamental grating that surrounds the shrine of an imam.

title: Hyderabad Woman Kills Husband By Injecting Toilet Cleaner, Arrested: Cops
content: A woman in Nizamabad district of Telangana allegedly killed her husband by injecting toilet cleaner through a cannula when he was undergoing medical treatment, police said on Monday.

title: 70 Applied For Citizenship In Assam Under New law, 6 Got It: Government
content: In yet another reply on the first day of the budget session, the Assam government said 1,72,673 foreigners have been detected in the state so far, while 31,786 have been deported.

title: Celina Jaitly To NDTV On Her Comeback After Years Of Harrowing Abuse And Grief
content: "My craft is what has stood by me through all my agonies and ecstasies of life. And I think this is my way of rescuing myself," Celina Jaitly told NDTV

title: 4 Dead As Pune Rains Trigger Landslides
content: Four persons, including three members of a family, were killed in a landslide and wall collapse, while two others were swept away in separate rain-related incidents as heavy showers battered Pune district on Monday.

title: El Niño to dent India’s wind, hydropower output: study
content: The Centre for Research on Energy and Clean Air (CREA) projects that weaker wind and hydropower output, combined with rising demand for air conditioning, could open a generation gap of nearly 18 TWh (terawatt hour) over a period of one year to June 2027

title: Restore M.K. Stalin’s name in Nemmeli plaque, urges DMK
content: Former DMK Minister T.M. Anbarasan alleged the plaque had been covered with a cloth ahead of Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay’s visit to the plant for an inspection

title: Congress targets Modi on foreign policy, cites passport decline and ‘silence’ on Gaza
content: Congress alleges that the government policies for passport have hurt India’s global reputation; also calls out PM’s silence on ‘Israel’s genocide in Gaza’

title: Chennai Corporation defers new infrastructure projects, cancels tenders worth more than ₹600 crore
content: An official says the tender cancellations have been implemented mainly to stagger expenditure and improve planning. The civic body has also not released the annual ward maintenance funds for civic repairs and sanitation, leaving many buildings, including UPHCs, in a damaged state

title: Mumbai rains | Rain severs Mumbai-Pune road link, three killed in landslide; Opposition slams govt.
content: The State government deployed a 30-member team of the National Disaster Response Force in Pune district, where a landslide occurred at Patan village in Maval taluka

title: NIA names LeT founder Hafiz Saeed as accused in Pahalgam terror attack
content: The charge sheet provides details of Pakistan’s conspiracy, Saeed’s role, and supporting evidence collected by the NIA in the case; LeT and TRF have been charged as legal entities for their “role in planning, facilitating, and executing the Pahalgam terror attack”

title: Jaishankar to launch campaign for India’s non-permanent 2028-29 UNSC seat as UN politics heats up
content: The bid will require substantial diplomatic mobilisation by India as Tajikistan will be the other candidate for the seat meant for the Asia-Pacific region

title: Username feature row: Govt gives WhatsApp 3 more days to reply to notice
content: WhatsApp has given the govt three more days to respond to its notice over the proposed username feature . the feature could increase online fraud, phishing, impersonation and so-called "digital arrest" scams . initial deadline expired last week .

title: For hours, Pune cut off from Mumbai as rain hits highways, buses and railway tracks
content: the first major disruption occurred around 3am when a landslide slammed into railway tracks between Monkey Hill and Thakurwadi . by Monday evening, 26 trains were cancelled, including the Deccan Queen, Deccan Express, Indrayani Express, Intercity Express and Sinhagad Express . another 47 trains had to be diverted .

title: CNG, EV make up record 40% car sales in June
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: TTP claims responsibility for killing Pakistan Air Force officer in Islamabad
content: the suspect, now arrested, has been identified as Saad Abbasi (22) the terrorist group claims responsibility for the killing . the slain officer was attached to the intelligence directorate that recently provided the coordinates in the recent drone strike in Afghanistan .
